# Technical Manual Extract: Pages 72-74
**Eversys S.A. Legacy User Manual - Published on 29.06.2022**

## Section 6.4: Product & Keys Menu (Manager Access)

### Technical Specifications

#### Hot Water Quantity Settings:
- **Steam Boiler Dispensing**: Set in seconds
- **Coffee Boiler Dispensing**: Set in ticks (controlled by flowmeter)
- **Adjustment Range**: 0 to 1000 ticks
- **Conversion**: Ticks are approximately equal to ml

#### Milk Quantity Settings:
- **Unit**: Set in seconds
- **Adjustment Range**: 0 to 100 seconds

### User Interface Elements and Controls

The interface shows numbered control elements (1-10) with the following functions:

#### Primary Controls (1-7):
1. **Key Name**: Press to type in a new name (maximum 13 characters)
2. **Key Icon**: Press to select another product picture
3. **Water Quantity**: Press to modify the number of ticks or seconds (adjust with the slider or up/down keys)
4. **Learn Quantity**:
   - a. Press Learn Quantity to dispense a coffee
   - b. When the desired level is in the cup, press the same button to stop the drink. This memorises the number of ticks (or seconds)
5. **Browse to the next menu**
6. **Milk Sequence**: Press one of the line under the Milk sequence to open available settings (only for milk products and if you have a fridge)
7. **Powder Sequence**: Press the line under the Powder sequence to open available settings (only for powder products and if you have a choco module or a powder unit)

#### Secondary Controls (8-10):
8. **Milk Quantity**: (only for milk products and if you have a fridge), press to modify the numbers of seconds or Learn Quantity
9. **Water Quantity**: (only for powder products and if you have a choco module or a powder unit), press to modify the quantity (in ml)
10. **Product Test**

### Product Test Requirements:
- **Minimum Test Runs**: Ensure that at least 6 beverages are run using Product Test
- **Purpose**: To achieve the desired coffee and extraction time
- **Verification**: That the grinder is delivering the correct amount of coffee for the set cake thickness
- **Adjustment Reference**: To adjust see Bean & Grinder menu [78]

### Product Type Configurations

#### Section 6.4.1: EverfoamTM and Auto Foam Products
**Controllable Parameters**:
- Stop Temperature
- Foam Texture

**Visual Interface Elements**:
- Products and Keyboard interface shown
- Key Name: Everfoam
- Stop Temperature: 65 [°C]
- Foam Texture: 50
- Product Test button (highlighted in red)

#### Section 6.4.2: Hot Water Products
**Controllable Parameters**:
- Water Quantity
- Water Temperature

**Visual Interface Specifications**:
- Products and Keyboard interface displayed
- Key Name: Hot Water
- Water Quantity: 10.0 [s]
- Water Temp.: 80 [°C]
- Product Test button (highlighted in red)

### Visual Interface Descriptions

#### Main Product Configuration Screen:
- Shows numbered interface elements (1-10)
- Multiple screenshot panels showing different configuration states
- "Products and Keyboard" header visible
- Various control buttons and settings displays
- Navigation arrows for menu browsing

#### EverfoamTM Configuration Screen:
- Black interface background
- "Products and Keyboard" title
- "1x" multiplier display
- Everfoam product icon
- Temperature and texture control sliders
- Product test functionality highlighted

#### Hot Water Configuration Screen:
- Similar black interface layout
- Hot Water product icon
- Quantity and temperature parameter controls
- Consistent navigation elements

### Hardware Dependencies

#### Milk Products Requirements:
- Fridge unit must be present
- Milk sequence settings only available with fridge

#### Powder Products Requirements:
- Choco module OR powder unit must be present
- Powder sequence settings dependent on module availability
- Water quantity measured in ml for powder products

### Reference Numbers and Cross-References:
- **Bean & Grinder Menu Reference**: [78]
- **Page Numbers**: 72-74 of 161
- **Document Version**: Legacy User Manual © Eversys S.A.
- **Publication Date**: 29.06.2022

### Technical Measurement Units:
- **Ticks**: Approximately equal to milliliters (ml)
- **Time**: Seconds (s)
- **Temperature**: Degrees Celsius (°C)
- **Volume**: Milliliters (ml) for powder products
- **Character Limit**: Maximum 13 characters for key names
heading: 6.4. Product & Keys menu (Manager access)
matched_queries: product settings recipe parameters drink settings beverage quantity milk quantity milk temperature
product and keys menu water quantity milk quantity hot water quantity learn quantity
milk sequence popup milk quantity milk temperature foam setting beverage parameters

text:
# COMPLETE TECHNICAL MANUAL EXTRACTION - PAGES 58-60
## Section 5.9: Make a drink

### General Information
- **Manual**: Legacy User Manual © Eversys S.A.
- **Publication Date**: 29.06.2022
- **Page Range**: 58-60 of 161
- **Section**: Operation

### 5.9 Make a drink - Overview
- **Note**: It is not possible to outline all drink products in this manual. You can find an overview of the main products in the following chapters.
- **Settings Reference**: To change settings or display of the product buttons, refer to User rights in the Display menu [153].
- **Button Function**: All product buttons function as start/stop buttons.
- **Operation Method**: Press the button to start dispensing the product and, if necessary, to stop dispensing manually: press the same product button again.

### 5.9.1 Adjust the coffee spout height

#### Technical Specifications:
- **Maximum Cup Height**: 190 mm
- **Component**: Coffee spout (1)
- **Adjustment Method**: Move the coffee spout (1) up or down to adjust it according to cup height

#### Visual Content Description:
- Image shows coffee machine with adjustable spout mechanism
- Arrow indicators showing vertical movement (up/down) of spout
- Component labeled as (1) indicating the coffee spout

### 5.9.2 Dispense a single coffee product

#### Operation Instructions:
- **Button Function**: All product buttons function as start/stop buttons
- **Control Method**: Press the button to start dispensing the product and, if necessary, to stop dispensing manually: press the same product button again

#### Step-by-Step Procedure:
1. Adjust the coffee spout height [58]
2. Place a cup under the coffee outlet
3. Press one of the single coffee products available (e.g. Espresso)
4. Wait for the product to dispense

#### Visual Content Description:
- Espresso button icon shown in black square with coffee cup symbol
- Button labeled "Espresso"

### 5.9.3 Dispense a double coffee product

#### Operation Instructions:
- **Button Function**: All product buttons function as start/stop buttons
- **Control Method**: Press the button to start dispensing the product and, if necessary, to stop dispensing manually: press the same product button again

#### Step-by-Step Procedure:
1. Adjust the coffee spout height [58]
2. Place two cups under the coffee outlet
3. Press one of the double coffee products available (e.g. 2x Espresso)
4. Wait for the product to dispense

#### Visual Content Description:
- "2x Espresso" button icon shown in black square with double coffee cup symbol
- Button labeled "2x Espresso"

### 5.9.4 Dispense a hot water product

#### Technical Information:
- **Outlet Configuration**: Depending on machine settings and outputs available, the hot water product will be dispensed through the coffee outlet and/or the hot water outlet
- **Service Reference**: For more information, contact your service organisation

#### Operation Instructions:
- **Button Function**: All product buttons function as start/stop buttons
- **Control Method**: Press the button to start dispensing the product and, if necessary, to stop dispensing manually: press the same product button again

#### Step-by-Step Procedure:
1. Adjust the coffee spout height [58]
2. Place a cup under the coffee outlet or the hot water outlet
3. Press the hot water product
4. Wait for the product to dispense

#### Visual Content Description:
- "Hot Water" button icon shown in black square with water symbol

### 5.9.5 Dispense a product with steam

#### Safety Warning:
- **CRITICAL**: Never leave the steam wand immersed in the pitcher if not used

#### Operation Instructions:
- **Button Function**: All product buttons function as start/stop buttons
- **Control Method**: Press the button to start dispensing the product and, if necessary, to stop dispensing manually: press the same product button again

#### Step-by-Step Procedure:
1. Before using the steam wand, press the purge button [53] to evacuate air from possible condensation
2. Insert the steam wand in the milk frothing pitcher (filled to one third with fresh cold milk)
3. Press one of the steam buttons to dispense a steam product (e.g. to make milk foam or coffee art)
4. Wait for the product to dispense
5. Purge and wipe the steam wand [53]

#### Visual Content Description:
- Two control panel interface screenshots showing various beverage options
- Image of milk frothing pitcher with steam wand inserted
- Component labeled as (1) indicating steam-related control
- Hand gesture illustrations showing button pressing actions
- Control panels show multiple beverage icons including coffee, steam, and other drink options

### 5.9.6 Dispense a powder product (e.g. choco) (machine option)

#### Operation Instructions:
- **Button Function**: All product buttons function as start/stop buttons
- **Control Method**: Press the button to start dispensing the product and, if necessary, to stop dispensing manually: press the same product button again

#### Step-by-Step Procedure:
1. Adjust the coffee spout height [58]
2. Place a cup under the coffee outlet
3. Press one of the powder product (e.g. Choco)
4. Wait for the product to dispense

#### Visual Content Description:
- "Choco" button icon shown in black square with chocolate drink symbol
- Button labeled "Choco"

### Reference Numbers and Cross-References:
- [53] - Purge button reference
- [58] - Coffee spout height adjustment reference
- [153] - User rights in Display menu reference

### Technical Components Identified:
- Coffee spout (1)
- Coffee outlet
- Hot water outlet
- Steam wand
- Milk frothing pitcher
- Purge button
- Product buttons (Espresso, 2x Espresso, Hot Water, Choco)

### Machine Options:
- Powder product dispensing (noted as machine option)
- Multiple outlet configurations for hot water
heading: 5.9. Make a drink
matched_queries: product settings recipe parameters drink settings beverage quantity milk quantity milk temperature
product and keys menu water quantity milk quantity hot water quantity learn quantity
milk sequence popup milk quantity milk temperature foam setting beverage parameters

# Page 69 - Settings Menu - Use of Software Menu

## Section 6.2. Use of the software menu

### Interface Diagram Description
The page shows a software interface screenshot labeled "Products and Keyboard" with numbered callouts (1-10) identifying different interface elements.

**Visual Interface Elements:**
- Left panel showing product selection with "1x" and "2x" options
- Coffee cup icon labeled "Cappuccino"
- Product settings table with parameters and values
- Right panel showing control buttons and product icons
- Center area displaying "50 [tick]" with slider controls
- Navigation arrows and menu controls

**Product Settings Table (visible in left panel):**
- Product Type: Cappuccino
- Key Name: Cappuccino  
- Bean Hopper: Right
- Water Quantity: 50 [tick]
- Cake Thickness: 14.0 [mm]
- Tamping: 20 [kg]
- Pre-Infusion: 0.8 [s]
- Relax Time: 2.0 [s]
- Second Tamping: 2.0 [mm]

### Numbered Interface Elements and Functions:

**1. Select a product.**

**2. If needed, select another icon.**

**3. Press a sub-menu to display its settings.**

**4. Slider: Slide left or right to adjust settings.**

**5. Up/down keys: Use these arrows for fine adjustments.**

**6. Product Test, use this button to test the product and avoid going back to the main screen.**

**7. Learn Quantity, use this button to adjust the quantity of water or milk depending on the product.**

**8. Browse menu.**

**9. Browse layers.**

**10. Navigate to previous menu.**

### Technical Parameters Visible:
- Water Quantity: 50 [tick]
- Cake Thickness: 14.0 [mm]
- Tamping: 20 [kg]
- Pre-Infusion: 0.8 [s]
- Relax Time: 2.0 [s]
- Second Tamping: 2.0 [mm]

### Document Information:
- Page 69 of 161
- Published on 29.06.2022
- Legacy User Manual © Eversys S.A.

### Interface Features:
- Product selection with 1x and 2x multiplier options
- Cappuccino product type selected
- Right bean hopper designation
- Slider control showing "50 [tick]" value
- Product Test and Learn Quantity buttons prominently displayed
- Multiple coffee product icons in grid layout on right side
- Navigation controls for menu browsing and layer navigation
heading: 6.2. Use of the software menu

# Technical Manual Extract: Pages 150-157
## Eversys S.A. Legacy User Manual - User Rights Section

### Document Information
- **Document**: Legacy User Manual © Eversys S.A.
- **Publication Date**: Published on 29.06.2022
- **Pages**: 150-157 of 161
- **Software Version**: V3.17 (illustrations)
- **Display Style**: Monochrome style (illustrations)
- **Section**: 11.3 User Rights (Appendix)

---

## 11.3. User Rights

### User Level Definitions
The table below describes the menu user level rights according to the manufacturer's default settings when the machine is shipped.

**User Levels:**
- **User** (basic user)
- **Manager** (super user)

**Technical Notes:**
- Illustrations show software V3.17
- Illustrations show monochrome style

---

## 11.3.1. User Rights in the Statistic / History Menu

### Visual Interface Description
**Statistics | History Menu Display:**
- Black interface with white text "Statistics | History"
- Three main icons displayed:
  1. **Product Counter** - Bar chart icon with ascending bars
  2. **Machine Counter** - Multiple bar chart icon
  3. **History** - Calendar-style icon showing "18/09/2019"
- White navigation arrow (back button) at bottom left

### User Rights Table - Statistics/History Menu

| Parameter | Default | User | Super user | Advanced user | More information |
|-----------|---------|------|------------|---------------|-----------------|
| **Product Counter** | - | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Product counter menu [70] |
| • Day Counter | - | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Product counter menu [70] |
| • Period Counter | - | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Product counter menu [70] |
| • Total Counter | - | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Product counter menu [70] |
| **Machine Counter** | - | - | - | ✓ | - |
| **History** | - | - | - | ✓ | - |
| • Product History | - | - | - | ✓ | - |
| • Error History | - | - | - | ✓ | - |
| • Cleaning History | - | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | - |
| • Rinse History | - | - | - | ✓ | - |
| • Service History | - | - | - | ✓ | - |

---

## 11.3.2. User Rights in the Product & Keys Menu

**Description:** The tables below describe the default settings for a cappuccino. User level rights are similar for any other products.

### Visual Interface Description - Page 1/2
**Products and Keyboard Interface:**
- Black interface displaying "Products and Keyboard"
- **Product Selection**: Shows "1x" and "2x" buttons with cappuccino icon
- **Product Name**: "Cappuccino"
- **Water Quantity Slider**: Shows "50 [tick]" with range 10-750
- **Product Test** and **Learn Quan...** buttons visible
- **Parameter Display**:
  - Product Type: Cappuccino
  - Key Name: Cappuccino  
  - Bean Hopper: Right
  - Water Quantity: 50 [tick]
  - Cake Thickness: 14,0 [mm]
  - Tamping: 20 [kg]
  - Pre-Infusion: 0,8 [s]
  - Relax Time: 2,0 [s]
  - Second Tamping: 2,0 [mm]
- **Status Bar**: Shows "Coffee Boiler: 0°C", "Steam Wand: 0°C", "0,0 lt/bar/h", "0,0 bar", "0CFG"
- **Page Indicator**: "1 ▷" (Page 1 of 2)

### User Rights Table - Product & Keys Menu (Page 1/2)

---

## 11.3.3. User Rights in the Display Menu

### Visual Interface Description - Page 1/3
**Display Menu Interface:**

- **Two main buttons**:
  - "Import Screen" (green button)
  - "Import Language" (blue button)
- **Parameter List Displayed**:
  - Language: English (English, EN)
  - Date and Time: 22. Oct 2021, 10:40:23
  - Screen Style: Monochrome
  - Number of Display Layer: 2
  - Automatic Go Back To Layer 1: Yes
  - Number of Keys per Layer: 15
  - Screensaver: Off
  - Front Lighting: Mix
  - Front Lighting Brightness: 90
  - Display Brightness: 90
- **Page Indicator**: "1/3"

| **Display Brightness** | 90 | - | - | ✓ | Contact service organisation. |

### User Rights Table - Display Menu (Page 2/3)

## 11.3.4. User Rights in the Beans & Grinder Menu on Légacy Machines

**Technical Note:** 
> Illustrations show picture of Cameo machines. Légacy machine screens will be based on this philosophy.

### Visual Interface Description - Grinder Adjustment
**Grinder Adjustment Interface:**
- Black interface with "Grinder Adjustment" header and gear icon
- **Dual Hopper Layout**:
  - **Left Hopper Section**:
    - Display shows "0" at top
    - "Finer (-)" button (orange/red outline)
    - "Coarser (+)" button (orange/red outline)
    - "Reset" button at bottom
    - "#1" indicator
    - "Grind Hopper Left" label
  - **Right Hopper Section**:
    - Display shows "0" at top  
    - "Finer (-)" button (orange/red outline)
    - "Coarser (+)" button (orange/red outline)
    - "Reset" button at bottom
    - "#2" indicator
    - "Grind Hopper Right" label
- **Central Image**: Coffee machine representation with green and blue hopper indicators
- White navigation arrow (back button) at bottom left

### User Rights Table - Grinder Adjustment

| Parameter | Default | User (Password protected) | Super user | Advanced user | More information |
|-----------|---------|---------------------------|------------|---------------|-----------------|
| **Grind Hopper Left** | - | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Bean & Grinder menu [78] |
| **Finer (-) [1/100 mm]** | 0 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Bean & Grinder menu [78] |
| **Coarser (+) [1/100 mm]** | 0 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Bean & Grinder menu [78] |
| **Grind Hopper Right** | - | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Bean & Grinder menu [78] |
| **Finer (-) [1/100 mm]** | 0 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Bean & Grinder menu [78] |
| **Coarser (+) [1/100 mm]** | 0 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | Bean & Grinder menu [78] |

---

## Technical Specifications Summary

### Parameter Value Ranges and Units
- **Water Quantity**: 10-750 [tick], Default: 50
- **Cake Thickness**: 14,0 [mm]
- **Tamping**: 20 [kg]
- **Pre-infusion**: 0,8 [s]
- **Relax Time**: 2,0 [s] 
- **Second Tamping**: 2,0 [mm]
- **Bypass Quantity**: 0 [%]
- **Coffee Cycles**: 1 [#]
- **Milk Delay Time**: 0,0 [s]
- **Milk Quantity**: 9,0 [s]
- **Foam Texture**: 80
- **Water Quantity (Powder)**: 100 [ml]
- **Powder Density**: 5,0 +/- 1 [g/100ml]
- **Display Layers**: 2
- **Keys per Layer**: 15
- **Front Lighting Brightness**: 90
- **Display Brightness**: 90
- **Grinder Adjustment**: 0 [1/100 mm] increments

### Reference Menu Numbers
- [70] Product counter menu
- [72] Product & Keys menu (Manager access)
- [75] Display menu  
- [76] Import Screen/Language menu
- [78] Bean & Grinder menu

### Default Settings
- **Language**: English (EN)
- **Date Format**: DD.MM.YYYY
- **Screen Style**: Monochrome
- **Milk Temperature**: Warm
- **Appearance**: White on Top
- **Screensaver**: Off
- **Front Lighting**: Mix
- **Bean Hopper**: TBC (To Be Confirmed)

text:
# Page 39 - Product Buttons (Default)

## Section 3.3. Product buttons (default)

### Complete Product Button Configuration Table

**Left Column Products:**

1. **Ristretto**
   - Visual: Black button with white cup icon and "Ristretto" text

2. **2 x Espresso**  
   - Visual: Black button with white double cup icon and "2x Espresso" text

3. **Coffee**
   - Visual: Black button with white cup icon and "Coffee" text

4. **Coffee powder - Decaf (Special product)**
   - Visual: Black button with white cup icon with spoon symbol and "Coffee" text

5. **Americano (long black)**
   - Visual: Black button with white tall glass icon and "Americano" text

6. **Coffee pot**
   - Visual: Black button with white coffee pot icon and "Coffee Pot" text

7. **Filter coffee**
   - Visual: Black button with white cup icon and "Filter Coffee" text

8. **Hot water (Tea)**
   - Visual: Black button with white cup icon and "Hot Water" text

9. **Choco**
   - Visual: Black button with white tall glass icon and "Choco" text

## Section 6.6. Bean & Grinder menu

### Access Control Information
- **Display Access**: Button display depends on settings
- **Default Settings**: Limited to service engineer access
- **User Rights Reference**: Display menu [153]

### Grinder Adjustment Menu Specifications
- **Manual Adjustment Capability**: Grind size adjustment by **1/100 mm** increments
- **Adjustment Controls**:
  - Press **finer (-)** to decrease grind size
  - Press **coarser (+)** to increase grind size

## Section 6.6.1. Légacy
- **Status**: TO BE ADDED

### Visual Content Description
- **Image**: Large circular "no camera" icon (camera symbol with diagonal line through it) indicating missing visual content or placeholder for future addition

### Technical Specifications - Extraction Parameters
- **Grinder Setting Method**: Adjust grinder either finer or coarser to increase or decrease extraction time
- **Target Extraction Specifications**:
  - **Volume per espresso**: 25 ml each
  - **Quantity**: Two espressos
  - **Target extraction time range**: **21 to 23 seconds**
  - **Average extraction time**: 21 to 23 seconds for two espressos at 25 ml each

- Average extraction time for two espressos at 25 ml each is 21 to 23 seconds."

# COMPLETE EXTRACTION - Pages 136-137: Grinder Options

## Document Information
- **Source**: Legacy User Manual © Eversys S.A.
- **Publication Date**: Published on 29.06.2022
- **Page Range**: Page 136 of 161 - Page 137 of 161
- **Section**: 9.7. Grinder(s) option
- **Chapter**: Options

## 9.7. Grinder(s) option

### Key Information Notice
The installation of the powder unit option reduces the number of grinder option(s).
For more information about powder unit option, refer to Powder unit option [138].

## 9.7.1. 1 x grinder (Position 2)

### Specifications:
- **Configuration**: 1 x grinder
- **Position**: Position 2
- **Bean Hopper Capacity**: 1 x 1 kg bean hopper
- **Storage**: Large cups storage panel

### Visual Description:
- Left image shows physical coffee machine with single grinder unit and bean hopper on top, with cup storage visible
- Right image shows schematic diagram with position "2" marked in the upper section

## 9.7.2. 2 x grinders (Position 1 and 2)

### Specifications:
- **Configuration**: 2 x grinders
- **Positions**: Position 1 and Position 2
- **Bean Hopper Capacity**: 2 x 1 kg bean hoppers
- **Storage**: Cups storage panel

### Visual Description:
- Left image shows coffee machine with two grinder units and bean hoppers
- Right image shows schematic diagram with positions "1" and "2" marked, with "2" in upper section and "1" in lower section

## 9.7.3. 2 x grinders (Position 2 and 3)

### Specifications:
- **Configuration**: 2 x grinders
- **Positions**: Position 2 and Position 3
- **Bean Hopper Capacities**: 
  - 1 x 0.5 kg bean hopper
  - 1 x 1 kg bean hopper
- **Storage**: Cups storage panel

### Visual Description:
- Left image shows coffee machine with two grinder configuration
- Right image shows schematic diagram with positions "2" and "3" marked in upper section

## 9.7.4. 3 x grinders

### Specifications:
- **Configuration**: 3 x grinders
- **Positions**: Position 1, Position 2, Position 3
- **Bean Hopper Capacities**:
  - 1 x 0.5 kg bean hopper
  - 2 x 1 kg bean hoppers
- **Storage**: Small cups storage panel

### Visual Description:
- Left image shows coffee machine with three grinder units
- Right image shows schematic diagram with positions "1", "2", and "3" marked, with "3" and "2" in upper section, "1" in lower section

## 9.7.5. 4 x grinders

### Specifications:
- **Configuration**: 4 x grinders
- **Positions**: Position 1, Position 2, Position 3, Position 4
- **Bean Hopper Capacities**:
  - 2 x 0.5 kg bean hoppers
  - 2 x 1 kg bean hoppers
- **Storage**: Very small cups storage panel

### Visual Description:
- Left image shows coffee machine with four grinder units (maximum configuration)
- Right image shows schematic diagram with all four positions marked: "3" and "2" in upper section, "4" and "1" in lower section

## Technical Patterns and Relationships

### Bean Hopper Capacity Distribution:
- **1 grinder**: 1 kg total capacity
- **2 grinders (Pos 1&2)**: 2 kg total capacity (2 x 1 kg)
- **2 grinders (Pos 2&3)**: 1.5 kg total capacity (1 x 0.5 kg + 1 x 1 kg)
- **3 grinders**: 2.5 kg total capacity (1 x 0.5 kg + 2 x 1 kg)
- **4 grinders**: 3 kg total capacity (2 x 0.5 kg + 2 x 1 kg)

### Cup Storage Capacity Relationship:
- **1 grinder**: Large cups storage panel
- **2 grinders**: Cups storage panel (standard)
- **3 grinders**: Small cups storage panel
- **4 grinders**: Very small cups storage panel

### Position Layout Pattern:
- **Upper positions**: 2, 3 (horizontal arrangement)
- **Lower positions**: 1, 4 (horizontal arrangement)
- **Standard single position**: Position 2 (upper right)

### Reference Information:
- **Cross-reference**: Powder unit option [138]
- **Impact**: Powder unit installation reduces available grinder options

## Visual Equipment Details:
All configurations show the same base coffee machine unit with:
- Control panel with multiple buttons arranged in grid pattern
- Metallic/stainless steel construction
- Cup dispensing area at bottom
- Variable grinder and hopper mounting on top section
- Consistent schematic representation showing position numbering system

text:
# Page 32 - Technical Manual Content Extraction

## Document Header Information
- **Title**: Legacy User Manual
- **Publisher**: © Eversys S.A.
- **Publication Date**: 29.06.2022
- **Page**: 32 of 161
- **Section**: Description

## 2.5. Coffee Process Flow

### Overview Description
Depending on the process/product, the system will take suitable quantities of beans and grind them according to the product needs, supply them to the appropriate brew chamber and tamp according to settings. It will then brew and dispense the product according to your request.

### Visual Content Description
The page contains a detailed diagram showing the coffee brewing process with numbered components (1-5) against a red background. The diagram illustrates hands operating various components of the coffee machine system, including:
- Component 1: Grinding mechanism with beans falling into grinder
- Component 2: Portafilter handling and leveling
- Component 3: Temperature control system with gauges
- Component 4: Brewing process with espresso extraction
- Component 5: Milk frothing pitcher

### Technical Process Steps

#### 1. Grinding
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Burr Type**: Eversys-designed ceramic burrs
- **Motor Characteristics**: Powerful yet quiet motor
- **Heat Management**: Heat is cleverly channelled away from the coffee beans to maintain their quality via a set of fans
- **Control System**: Electronic control maintains consistency of product throughout the day, ensuring optimal extraction at all times

#### 2. e'Levelling and Tamping
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Levelling Process**: Automatic levelling initiates movement to the brewing chamber, creates vibration which enables powder in the chamber to spread more evenly
- **Brewing Chamber Capacity**: 24 gram brewing chamber
- **Tamping Control**: Electronic tamping to guarantee consistent quality
- **Function**: Facilitates single cycle large beverages
- **Barista Replication**: Movement is a replica of what the barista does - tap the portafilter
- **Result**: Leads to better, more consistent extraction

#### 3. Temperature Control
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Boiler Configuration**: Dedicated coffee boiler, separated from the water/steam boiler
- **Function**: Ensures productivity and temperature control
- **Quality Impact**: Essential parts of in-cup quality

#### 4. Brewing
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Extraction Method**: Reverse gravity extraction vertical infusion
- **Efficiency**: Optimises powder efficiency as the espresso is produced

#### 5. Milk Frothing
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Frothing Method**: Milk is frothed with air only in the one-step system
- **Quality Features**: Promoting flavour as well as texture
- **Experience**: Mirroring the Barista experience

#### 6. Dispensing
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Programming**: All products are pre-programmed to be dispensed in a consistent and efficient manner
- **Objectives**: Placing seamless productivity and quality at the forefront of the customer experience

### Footer Information
- **Document**: Legacy User Manual
- **Copyright**: © Eversys S.A.
- **Publication Date**: Published on 29.06.2022
- **Page Reference**: Page 32 of 161
heading: 2.5. Coffee process flow
